phase_outcome: COMPLETE — 6/6 changes archived; 5/6 goals fully MET, 1/6 (c006) MET on deliverables with an honestly disclosed partial gate (final /healthz criterion blocked by an unrelated, filed bug)
summary: Phase re-specced its own premise correctly when assessment found v1.0.0 was already tagged (arm64-only, broken) and CI had never passed. All 6 re-specced defect-driven changes shipped: pg_cron amd64 fix, realtime fail-closed, CI verification (0 waits — already green), vulnerability disclosure channel, doc reconciliation, and a genuinely-executed backup/restore + upgrade guide. Two P0 bugs were discovered (not assumed) while executing the restore: vault.secrets excluded from pg_dump, and flint_meta.views() missing breaks gateway startup for every operator. Both filed as follow-ups, not fixed in-phase (correctly out of scope) and not silently ignored.

open_release_blocker: v1.0.0 re-tag vs v1.0.1 — unresolved across 5 consecutive stages (assess, analyze, spec, plan, reflect). Current v1.0.0 tag predates the amd64 fix; GitHub Release has zero published assets, so nothing external is pinned to the broken state. This is a human decision, not a KBD one.
